subject: science
title: science_12.json
description: Grade 4 Science Assessment - Day 12: Forms of Energy and Conservation

questions.q: What type of energy is stored inside a battery, a slice of pizza, or a wooden log?
questions.hint: This energy is held inside the bonds of matter and is released during chemical reactions or digestion.
questions.explanation: Chemical energy is a form of potential energy stored in the bonds of chemical compounds, which can be released during a reaction (like burning or digestion).

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of mechanical kinetic energy?
options: A bicycle rolling down a paved path
A flashlight sitting inside a drawer
A battery pack waiting to be used
A radio that is completely turned off
questions.answer: A bicycle rolling down a paved path
questions.hint: Look for the object that is actively moving.
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy is the energy an object possesses due to its motion. A rolling bicycle is actively moving, representing mechanical kinetic energy.

questions.q: What form of energy travels in waves and can be detected by the human eye?
questions.hint: The Sun, lightbulbs, and fireflies all produce this form of energy so we can see.
questions.explanation: Light energy (or radiant energy) is a type of electromagnetic radiation that is visible to human eyes.

questions.q: When you plug a toaster into a wall outlet and press the lever down to toast bread, what energy transformation takes place?
options: Electrical energy transforms into thermal energy.
Sound energy transforms into chemical energy.
Kinetic energy transforms into magnetic energy.
Nuclear energy transforms into mechanical energy.
questions.answer: Electrical energy transforms into thermal energy.
questions.hint: Think about what goes into the toaster through the cord, and what gets hot inside to cook the bread.
questions.explanation: The toaster uses electricity from the outlet (electrical energy) and passes it through wires that heat up (thermal energy) to toast the bread.

questions.q: The scientific rule stating that energy cannot be created or destroyed, but can only change from one form to another, is called the:
questions.hint: To 'conserve' means to save or keep the total amount the same.
questions.explanation: The Law of Conservation of Energy states that the total amount of energy in an isolated system remains constant; it can change forms but cannot be made from nothing or destroyed.

questions.q: What form of energy is created by the movement of electrons through a conductor?
questions.hint: This energy powers televisions, computers, and household appliances.
questions.explanation: Electrical energy is the power an electric current carries through the movement of tiny charged particles called electrons.

questions.q: Why does a metal spoon left inside a hot bowl of soup eventually become warm to the touch?
options: Thermal energy moves from the hot liquid into the cooler metal spoon.
The spoon absorbs light energy from the kitchen lamps.
The soup creates magnetic forces that heat up the metal.
The cold from the spoon is destroying the heat of the soup.
questions.answer: Thermal energy moves from the hot liquid into the cooler metal spoon.
questions.hint: Heat always travels spontaneously from warmer objects to cooler objects.
questions.explanation: Thermal energy transfers from an object with a higher temperature (soup) to an object with a lower temperature (spoon) via a process called conduction.

questions.q: Which resource is considered a renewable source of energy that will never run out?
questions.hint: Think about a source that is constantly replenished by nature's weather patterns.
questions.explanation: Renewable energy comes from natural resources that regenerate quickly, such as wind, sunlight, and water. Fossil fuels like coal and oil take millions of years to form.

questions.q: What unwanted form of energy is almost always generated as a byproduct when mechanical parts rub together in a machine?
questions.hint: Think about how your hands get warm when you rub them together quickly.
questions.explanation: Friction between moving parts opposes motion and converts some of the mechanical kinetic energy into thermal energy (heat), which is often lost to the surrounding air.

questions.q: What form of energy is produced by vibrations traveling through a medium, which we can hear with our ears?
questions.hint: A clapping hand or a ringing bell releases this type of kinetic wave energy.
questions.explanation: Sound energy is the movement of energy through a substance (like air or water) in waves, produced when an object vibrates.
